content: The drm-rust-next branch is closing soon as v6.18-rc6 is coming out this weekend. Does anyone have unapplied patches that they want to apply for this cycle?
For nova-core: I would like to get my remaining boot0/boot42 patches submitted. I can submit a v9 patchset within a few hours, to address the lingering issues found in v8 overnight.
I'm referring to: [PATCH v8 0/6] gpu: nova: add boot42 support for next-gen GPUs

I would like to apply the gsp init patches as well which Lyude and Alex have reviewed. Lyude just left a few more nits though, mostly cosmetic, can I submit a v6 by Monday since it’s the weekend? Or I could carve out some time Saturday if it helps the timelines.
Link: https://lore.kernel.org/all/20251114195552.739371-1-joelagnelf@nvidia.com/
The branch closes when 6.18-rc6 is out.

OK. I've posted this, which I believe is ready to merge, but @Alexandre Courbot needs to agree:

[PATCH v9 0/4] gpu: nova: add boot42 support for next-gen GPUs

I'll try to push what can be for Nova this evening JST. No stress if something doesn't make it by -rc6, we can push it as soon as the branch reopens. Meeting this or that kernel release is only important if there are cross-system dependencies, of which there isn't in the remaining patches.
Yes. This small series is not critical. I'm just hoping to have one less patchset to carry along while the branch is closed, not a huge deal though.
@Joel Fernandes The comments from Lyude look simple to address, if there is nothing else I'll just apply a fix tonight before pushing
@Alexandre Courbot that’ll be great, thanks a lot!
@Alice Ryhl I think we got everything we wanted on drm-rust-next for this cycle pushed!
The branch is now closed. I'm going to send the PR now.
Looks like we need a tweak for a conflict with the rust tree. Could I get a quick RB?
Reviewed and tested, thanks a lot!
Could someone on the nova side help me with a short bulleted list of what has changed in nova-core/nova?

Sure:

  • Introduction of bitfield! macro, with support for different storage sizes and custom visibility,
  • Depend on CONFIG_64BIT,
  • Introduction of safe converters between integer types for which the conversion is lossless,
  • GSP initialized up to fully booted state on Ampere,
  • Use more future-proof register for GPU identification,
  • Various simplifications and optimizations.
I'll send it tomorrow once I see a clean linux-next.
6.19-rc6 is coming out this weekend, so the drm-rust-next branch is closing for this cycle.
I think @Danilo Krummrich mentioned we would have one more week this cycle, am I mistaken?
There is one more -rc in Linus's tree this cycle, but I haven't heard anything about drm-next accepting PRs for one more week. It's drm-next that matters for drm-rust-next.
Sorry, I forgot to mention this, we can send the drm-rust-next PR one week later.
I talked to Dave about this a couple of days ago.
ah, that's great, in that case drm-rust-next can be open one more week
drm-rust-next is now closed for new features and will re-open once -rc1 is released.
drm-rust-next is now closed for new features and will re-open for new features once -rc1 is released; fixes for code that is currently in drm-rust-next can still be applied.

@Alice Ryhl could you pick up this series for the Tyr register macros for drm-rust-next? it still rebases without conflict and i've had no responses on v5. https://lore.kernel.org/rust-for-linux/20260409-b4-tyr-use-register-macro-v5-v5-0-8abfff8a0204@collabora.com/
Done.

Alice Ryhl said:

Done.

Thank you!
@Alice Ryhl could you add the FEAT_RENDER patch from @Laura Nao to drm-rust-next? https://lore.kernel.org/rust-for-linux/20260507080914.95478-1-laura.nao@collabora.com/
@Deborah Brouwer I already picked it up a few hours ago, when I went through my list of patches to deal with.
great thanks!
drm-rust-next was tagged for 7.2-rc1, the branch remains open as announced; drm-rust-next-fixes is now open for fixes targeting changes that are staged for 7.2-rc1 until 7.2-rc1 is released.
